fullName: National Electricity Transmission System Security and Quality of Supply Standard
issuer: NESO (on behalf of the SQSS Review Group)
authority: Referenced by the Grid Code and TO licences; the design standard for the GB transmission system
file: 03 - Standards/NETS Security and Quality of Supply Standards v2.11.pdf
summary: The planning and design standard for the GB transmission network. Specifies the security of supply criteria (how much redundancy the network must have), voltage and frequency performance, fault level limits, and design criteria for transmission reinforcements. It is what NESO and TOs use when deciding if the network can accommodate a new connection and, if not, what reinforcement is needed.
scope: Chapter 2 — Planning criteria for generation connections
Chapter 3 — Planning criteria for demand connections
Chapter 4 — Planning criteria for transmission-transmission interfaces
Chapter 5 — Operational criteria (steady-state, voltage, frequency, reserve)

keyParts.name: Generation secured capacity
keyParts.detail: The 'year-round' and 'peak' security criteria that determine how much generation the network must be designed to export or import.

keyParts.name: N-1 / N-1-1 criteria
keyParts.detail: Core transmission design rule: the system must withstand specified contingencies without loss of supply.

keyParts.name: Reactive and voltage criteria
keyParts.detail: Design voltage windows; MVAr planning for system stability.

keyParts.name: Fault level limits
keyParts.detail: Design fault levels at each substation; critical for switchgear ratings.

whoCares: Large transmission-connected customers (to understand likely reinforcement)
